content:
As a UX Engineer, I spend a lot of time thinking about how people learn software.

A new user needs guidance.

An experienced user needs speed.

But most interfaces treat both users exactly the same.

The UI remembers your data.

It remembers your settings.

But it doesn't remember what you already know.

What if interfaces could understand your familiarity and evolve with you?

Maybe the next generation of UI won't just respond to users.

Maybe it will learn from them.

Curious — what is one product you use every day that you wish understood you better?
